finding.recommendation: Keep the four reducer bodies per-mode inside deep-loop-workflows and import only shared runtime plumbing: atomic JSON/text writes, JSONL repair where already part of the mode contract, and exported context gate constants. Do not fold a parameterized reducer into deep-loop-runtime; the shared exact duplicate surface is only about 178 excess helper lines out of 4326 function-body lines, while buildRegistry/renderDashboard/state-shape logic is strongly mode-specific and belongs to workflows.
key_tradeoffs: Centralizing durability helpers removes drift without moving artifact semantics into the backend
Keeping reducers per-mode preserves byte-identical artifact behavior and per-mode state shapes
Adding a runtime text atomic helper is slightly more runtime API surface but avoids misusing JSON-only writeStateAtomic for markdown
Not adding JSONL tail repair to research/review avoids changing fail-closed corruption semantics
risks: Directly exporting constants from coverage-graph-signals.ts may drag graph/database dependencies into reducer startup
Changing research/review to repair JSONL tails would alter corrupted-state behavior
Runtime writeStateAtomic is JSON-only and does not create parent directories; the new text helper must preserve existing reducer behavior
Final paths depend on the unresolved deep-loop-workflows internal layout
open_questions: Will deep-loop-workflows preserve old subtree shapes or use modes/<mode>/ paths?
Should a CJS-safe runtime state-safety bridge be added, or should reducers continue using tsx to import runtime TypeScript?
Will research/review atomic output writes be included in the structural migration, or deferred to avoid any failure-mode semantic change?
